# Year Plan

This year is about building a system I can sustain, not chasing too many directions at once. I already have multiple apps, data systems, and ideas in motion. The goal now is to focus, refine, and connect them into something that actually produces results.

## Foundation: Health
Health is the foundation. I will:
- Maintain consistent sleep
- Train 3–4 times per week
- Keep routines simple and repeatable

This is not optional—it supports everything else.

## Primary Focus: Existing Apps
My main focus is improving my existing apps, especially in `apps/online/`.

- Limit focus to 1–2 apps at a time
- Make measurable improvements:
  - Usability
  - Features
  - Monetization
- Each work cycle should produce something tangible

## Secondary Focus: Passive Income
Passive income is structured experimentation.

- Work on only 1–2 ideas at a time
- Give each idea a defined window to prove itself
- Continue if it shows potential
- Drop it if it doesn’t

## Structure: LLC & Data Systems
I will organize my LLC and data systems into something stable and usable.

- Clean and document JSON and Firebase structures
- Use them as a control system (not just storage)
- Focus on clarity and consistency
- Avoid unnecessary rework

## Growth: Learning & Certifications
Learning is intentional and tied to outcomes.

- Choose skills that support apps or income
- Set a clear finish line for each certification or skill
- Avoid chasing perfection or switching directions without reason

## System Layers
My work is structured in layers:

- Surface → Portfolio site (presentation)
- Products → Apps (core value)
- Experiments → Project sites (`websites/*`)
- Backbone → Data systems (Firebase / JSON)

I will focus on one layer at a time while maintaining the others.

## Weekly Structure
Each week follows a simple structure:

- Majority of time → Existing apps
- Some time → Experiments
- Smaller portion → Organization and learning
- Health → Built into schedule (non-negotiable)

## Operating Rules
- Choose one primary app and define what “better” means
- Ship consistently instead of over-planning
- Limit new ideas to controlled experiments
- Use data systems as a source of truth
- Do not switch focus without a clear reason

## Goal
The goal is not to do everything.

The goal is to build momentum in the right places and carry it through the year.
